notes: This pilot normalizes SSDF objects conservatively to ControlObjective as the primary AppSec Core anchor.
Where AppSec Core v0 lacks a clean landing zone, the object is marked as core_gap rather than being forced into a misleading mapping.
Redirect-style SSDF objects preserve redirect semantics and inherit normalization only through their moved-to targets.
Practice/Mechanism/Artifact/EvidencePattern normalization is deferred until the objective-level line is proven stable.

items.source_title: Define Security Requirements for Software Development
items.rationale: Current AppSec Core v0 has no dedicated slice for cross-SDLC security requirements governance; the nearest landing zones remain too broad to claim a clean objective-level normalization.

nearest_core_anchors.name: Threat Modeling And Risk Governance Integrity
Secure Architecture Governance And Boundary Integrity

items.source_title: Implement Roles and Responsibilities
items.rationale: Role and responsibility semantics are intentionally under-modeled in AppSec Core v0, so this SSDF practice is preserved as a core-gap rather than forced into an access-control or ownership objective.

nearest_core_anchors.name: Threat Disposition, Risk Acceptance And Ownership
Identity And Access Control Integrity

items.source_title: Implement Supporting Toolchains
items.rationale: Supporting toolchains are primarily a build-execution integrity concern, with secondary alignment to reproducible validation evidence and centralized source accountability.

secondary_core_anchors.name: Reproducible Security Test Evidence And Build Traceability
Centralized Log Ingestion And Source Accountability

items.source_title: Define and Use Criteria for Software Security Checks
items.rationale: Criteria for software security checks land directly on risk-proportional security testing strategy, with release gating as a downstream supporting concern.

secondary_core_anchors.name: Pre-Promotion Security Gates And Staging Assurance

items.source_title: Implement and Maintain Secure Environments for Software Development
items.rationale: Secure development environments are closest to build-definition and execution integrity, with supporting ties to provenance integrity and protected operational identity.

secondary_core_anchors.name: Artifact Attestation And Provenance Integrity
Secret Handling And Operational Identity Integrity

items.source_title: Protect All Forms of Code from Unauthorized Access and Tampering
items.rationale: Protecting all forms of code is primarily an identity-and-access integrity concern, but it also supports trusted build-definition control.

secondary_core_anchors.name: Build Definition And Execution Integrity

items.source_title: Provide a Mechanism for Verifying Software Release Integrity
items.rationale: Verifying software release integrity lands directly on artifact attestation and provenance, with verified promotion as the immediate downstream release concern.

secondary_core_anchors.name: Verified Artifact Promotion

items.source_title: Archive and Protect Each Software Release
items.rationale: Archiving and protecting releases is primarily a deployment-traceability concern, with artifact provenance as supporting context.

secondary_core_anchors.name: Artifact Attestation And Provenance Integrity

items.source_title: Design Software to Meet Security Requirements and Mitigate Security Risks
items.rationale: Designing software to meet security requirements and mitigate risk spans both threat/risk governance and secure architecture governance, so the landing remains deliberately bounded.

primary_core_anchor.name: Threat Modeling And Risk Governance Integrity

secondary_core_anchors.name: Secure Architecture Governance And Boundary Integrity

items.source_title: Review the Software Design to Verify Compliance with Security Requirements and Risk Information
items.rationale: Reviewing software design for compliance is a direct architecture-review discipline, with threat-model lifecycle governance as secondary support.

primary_core_anchor.name: Architecture Review And Change Trigger Discipline

secondary_core_anchors.name: Independent Review And Threat Model Lifecycle Governance

items.source_title: Reuse Existing, Well-Secured Software When Feasible Instead of Duplicating Functionality
items.rationale: Reusing well-secured software aligns directly to controlled component sources, with dependency risk evaluation as supporting discipline.

secondary_core_anchors.name: Dependency Risk Evaluation And Policy Gating

items.source_title: Create Source Code by Adhering to Secure Coding Practices
items.rationale: Secure coding is broader than the current input-validation and service-integration slices; AppSec Core v0 does not yet expose a clean generic secure-coding landing zone.

nearest_core_anchors.name: Input Validation And Safe Failure Integrity
Integration Trust And Service Interaction Assurance

items.source_title: Configure the Compilation, Interpreter, and Build Processes to Improve Executable Security
items.rationale: Compilation, interpreter and build-process hardening lands directly on build-definition and execution integrity, with provenance integrity as supporting context.

secondary_core_anchors.name: Artifact Attestation And Provenance Integrity

items.source_title: Review and/or Analyze Human-Readable Code to Identify Vulnerabilities and Verify Compliance with Security Requirements
items.rationale: Reviewing or analyzing human-readable code maps directly to static-analysis signal governance, with finding triage and correction closure as secondary support.

primary_core_anchor.name: Static Analysis Signal Quality And Baseline Governance

secondary_core_anchors.name: Security Finding Triage And Correction Closure

items.source_title: Test Executable Code to Identify Vulnerabilities and Verify Compliance with Security Requirements
items.rationale: Testing executable code lands directly on staged dynamic validation, with deeper empirical assurance as the next supporting objective.

primary_core_anchor.name: Staged Dynamic Validation And Release Gate Discipline

secondary_core_anchors.name: Specialized Empirical Testing Depth And Regression Assurance

items.source_title: Configure Software to Have Secure Settings by Default
items.rationale: Secure-by-default configuration remains broader than the current AppSec Core v0 slices; the nearest architecture and protected-configuration anchors are still too indirect for a clean normalization.

nearest_core_anchors.name: Trust Boundary Clarity And Protected Data Flows
Secret Handling And Operational Identity Integrity

items.source_title: Identify and Confirm Vulnerabilities on an Ongoing Basis
items.rationale: Continuous vulnerability identification and confirmation lands directly on finding triage and correction closure, with logging/auditability as supporting context.

primary_core_anchor.name: Security Finding Triage And Correction Closure

secondary_core_anchors.name: Security Logging And Audit Trail Assurance

items.source_title: Assess, Prioritize, and Remediate Vulnerabilities
items.rationale: Assessing, prioritizing and remediating vulnerabilities is primarily a finding-triage discipline, with risk disposition and ownership as secondary support.

primary_core_anchor.name: Security Finding Triage And Correction Closure

secondary_core_anchors.name: Threat Disposition, Risk Acceptance And Ownership

items.source_title: Analyze Vulnerabilities to Identify Their Root Causes
items.rationale: Root-cause analysis is best represented as lifecycle governance of threat and vulnerability learning, but it still spans architecture review and finding correction.

primary_core_anchor.name: Independent Review And Threat Model Lifecycle Governance

secondary_core_anchors.name: Architecture Review And Change Trigger Discipline
Security Finding Triage And Correction Closure

items.source_title: Verify Third-Party Software Complies with Security Requirements

secondary_core_anchors.name: Dependency Risk Evaluation And Policy Gating
